text: What most people do not know is that this dance was first performed on stage at the "Moulin Rouge" in Paris.  What made it an immediate hit was the music, the girls, and the fact that the girls wore no underware.  Now, when you see this dance being performed today, just imagine what it would be liike if the dancers wore no panties.

text:
This little film clip is fantastic.  I've watched it two dozen times.  This is a high-spirited, high-kicking, athletic performance.  The guy in the top hat is a crack-up!  These girls are dancing and kicking like crazy, and he simply walks out on stage and offers a little challenge.  I love the period costumes. Compare this to any other Can Can video. This is the best! 
 Who would have thought that granny panties could be so naughty!
author: @ScottGLloyd

text: I bought this film on CD a few years back. The quality of the print is a little better, but not by much. Nonetheless, I still feel that this is the best Can Can on the internet, and probably ever put to film.
author: @ScottGLloyd

text: I agree this performance is splendid! But did you see the cancan scene from 1952 movie moulin rouge by john huston? text:
That is true, but when the can can was invented, it was changing. The mainstream places like the Moulin Rouge had the girls wear the new underwear - with a slit in it, for ease of use in relief. So who knew what you would see.

But some of the low dance halls elsewhere in Montmarte, well…
